notes.misattribution_rate_all: fraction of truly-induced events NOT labelled Induced-cascade. Under a temporal GT (window = gt_ref), corrected is low largely by label consistency; the CONTESTED rate is the non-tautological number (where a competing audit anchor makes the order decide).
notes.misattribution_rate_contested: restricted to GT events with an audit anchor within W: here the priority order genuinely decides Induced-cascade vs Direct-anchor, so a low corrected rate vs high naive/literal_may is real evidence the priority rule prevents causal misattribution (R2.12).
notes.induced_window_coverage: GT is the temporal window, so this is coverage, not independent detection; naive is 0 by construction.
notes.literal_may: reproduces the shipped May binary (Direct-first, no legit exception); submitted isolates ONLY the ordering vs corrected.
